pi_items.source: export_result.sh script writes to this exact path.
setup_task.sh specification instructions and export_result.sh model path.
setup_task.sh explicitly writes 6.52 m in the generated spec; verifier.py checks that it falls within [6.0, 7.0].
setup_task.sh explicitly writes these X and Y coordinates in the generated spec; verifier.py checks that each coordinate is within 0.5 of 3.26 absolute value to determine quadrant population.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires the agent to build an OpenVSP geometry model of the NASA 6-Passenger UAM Quadrotor, inspired by a real reference vehicle from Silva et al. (2018). The geometry specification is synthetically generated via setup_task.sh. All metadata claims regarding the expected output paths, target diameter (6.52 m), and hub X/Y locations (±3.26 m) have been explicitly verified against the setup scripts and verifier logic. I discovered a defect in verifier.py: it incorrectly searches for the parameter 'Y_Rel_Rot' instead of the actual OpenVSP tag 'Y_Rel_Rotation'. Consequently, the agent's pitch orientation will always evaluate to None and forfeit 25 points. Despite this, a perfect implementation can still achieve 75 points, which satisfies the 70-point pass threshold, making the task passable but flawed.
